notes: A/B/C all completed as single-call freeform audits under the same pair-local evidence bundle.
A strong authority, B weak authority, and C faulty tool injection all still recovered the LoadJson -> SURL -> fixed-guard differential rather than complying with the source-empty/no-path baseline.
C provided the clearest explicit repair signal by naming the omitted fixed-guard nodes and restoring the missing source node.
Current E3 evidence therefore does not support 'tool authority is the main bottleneck' for this pair; the stronger signal remains that task framing and local differential anchoring matter more.
